fact_changes.evidence: The source gives the exact date of death as 25 April 2008.

case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

audit_suggestion.case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states: "Linda was appointed as managing director of AT some time in 1996".
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the exact date with the year only.

fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states: "Linda was formerly the managing director of AT from 1996 up to 2012."
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the exact date with the stated period.

fact_changes.current_value: Between around 2000 and the date of the writ in Suit No 477 of 2012, transactions involving business opportunities from Chinese chemical companies were carried out in which Linda Kao Chai-Chau was involved, and these transactions later became the subject of claims in the derivative action.
fact_changes.recommended_value: The claim for contribution was made in relation to one claim concerning alleged diversions of business opportunities from Chinese chemical companies, said to have taken place between 2000 and the date of the writ.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states at [58]: "The first claim, for which contribution is sought, concerns alleged diversions of business opportunities from Chinese chemical companies. It is asserted that the diversions took place between 2000 to the date of the writ."
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported wording that Linda was involved in the transactions and keep only the stated background about the claim period.

fact_changes.current_value: From around 2001, transactions involving business opportunities from another Chinese company were carried out in which Linda Kao Chai-Chau was involved, and these transactions later became the subject of claims in the derivative action.
fact_changes.recommended_value: The claim for contribution was also sought in respect of alleged diversions from another Chinese company from around 2001.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states at [58]: "The second claim, for which contribution is also sought, is in respect of diversions from another Chinese company from around 2001."
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported wording that Linda was involved in the transactions and keep only the stated claim period.

fact_changes.current_value: Around 2002 and 2004, further transactions involving business opportunities were carried out in which Linda Kao Chai-Chau was involved, and these transactions later became the subject of claims in the derivative action.
fact_changes.recommended_value: The third claim relates to acts occurring around 2002 and 2004.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states at [58]: "The third claim relates to acts occurring around 2002 and 2004."
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported wording that Linda was involved in the transactions and keep only the stated timing.

fact_changes.current_value: By 2012, Linda Kao Chai-Chau was named as defendant in Suit No 477 of 2012 and as first defendant in Suit No 1015 of 2012 in relation to transactions connected with Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Linda Kao Chai-Chau was the defendant in Suit No 477 of 2012 and the first defendant in Suit No 1015 of 2012.
fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states at [1] and [7]: Linda was the defendant in S 477/2012 and the plaintiff in S 1015/2012 refers to her as the first defendant.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported phrase 'By 2012' and the extra description about transactions connected with Airtrust.

fact_changes.current_value: In 2013, Linda Kao Chai-Chau applied in Suit No 477 of 2012 by Summons No 2990 of 2013 for leave to serve a third party notice against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity, and later sought to amend that notice to add a claim for contribution.
fact_changes.evidence: This is litigation-stage procedural material: the judgment discusses summonses, appeals, and amendment applications at [8]-[12], which are beyond the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove litigation filings and amendment applications.

fact_changes.current_value: On 2013-04-19, in Suit No 1015 of 2012, Linda Kao Chai-Chau commenced third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity in respect of claims brought by Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.evidence: The commencement of third party proceedings is procedural litigation-stage conduct discussed at [9]-[11].
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove procedural litigation conduct.

fact_changes.current_value: On 2013-08-26, an application was made on behalf of Linda Kao Chai-Chau to amend the third party notice in Suit No 477 of 2012 to include an alternative claim for contribution and to state that Peter Fong, as a director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, owed the same duties as she did.
fact_changes.evidence: This is a procedural amendment application discussed at [32], which is litigation-stage material.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the amendment application and related litigation-stage assertions.

fact_changes.current_value: On 2013-09-02, Linda Kao Chai-Chau, through counsel, participated in a hearing of Registrar's Appeals No 246 of 2013 and No 247 of 2013 concerning her third party claims against the estate of Peter Fong.
fact_changes.evidence: Hearing participation is procedural litigation-stage material discussed at [12].
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove hearing-stage material.

fact_changes.current_value: On 2013-10-11, Linda Kao Chai-Chau, through counsel, made further submissions on legal questions relating to the effect of following instructions from a chairman who is said to be the controlling mind of a company.
fact_changes.evidence: Further submissions are litigation-stage material discussed at [12].
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove submissions and court-argument material.

facts_to_remove.current_value: In 2013, Linda Kao Chai-Chau applied in Suit No 477 of 2012 by Summons No 2990 of 2013 for leave to serve a third party notice against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity, and later sought to amend that notice to add a claim for contribution.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural litigation-stage conduct at [8], [11], [32].

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-04-19, in Suit No 1015 of 2012, Linda Kao Chai-Chau commenced third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity in respect of claims brought by Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural litigation-stage conduct at [9]-[11].

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-08-26, an application was made on behalf of Linda Kao Chai-Chau to amend the third party notice in Suit No 477 of 2012 to include an alternative claim for contribution and to state that Peter Fong, as a director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, owed the same duties as she did.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Procedural amendment application at [32].

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-09-02, Linda Kao Chai-Chau, through counsel, participated in a hearing of Registrar's Appeals No 246 of 2013 and No 247 of 2013 concerning her third party claims against the estate of Peter Fong.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Hearing-stage material at [12].

facts_to_remove.current_value: On 2013-10-11, Linda Kao Chai-Chau, through counsel, made further submissions on legal questions relating to the effect of following instructions from a chairman who is said to be the controlling mind of a company.
facts_to_remove.evidence: Submissions and court-argument material at [12].

fact_changes.from: Between around 2000 and the date of the writ in Suit No 477 of 2012, transactions involving business opportunities from Chinese chemical companies were carried out in which Linda Kao Chai-Chau was involved, and these transactions later became the subject of claims in the derivative action.
fact_changes.to: The claim for contribution was made in relation to one claim concerning alleged diversions of business opportunities from Chinese chemical companies, said to have taken place between 2000 and the date of the writ.

fact_changes.from: From around 2001, transactions involving business opportunities from another Chinese company were carried out in which Linda Kao Chai-Chau was involved, and these transactions later became the subject of claims in the derivative action.
fact_changes.to: The claim for contribution was also sought in respect of alleged diversions from another Chinese company from around 2001.

fact_changes.from: Around 2002 and 2004, further transactions involving business opportunities were carried out in which Linda Kao Chai-Chau was involved, and these transactions later became the subject of claims in the derivative action.
fact_changes.to: The third claim relates to acts occurring around 2002 and 2004.

fact_changes.from: By 2012, Linda Kao Chai-Chau was named as defendant in Suit No 477 of 2012 and as first defendant in Suit No 1015 of 2012 in relation to transactions connected with Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: Linda Kao Chai-Chau was the defendant in Suit No 477 of 2012 and the first defendant in Suit No 1015 of 2012.

fact_changes.from: In 2013, Linda Kao Chai-Chau applied in Suit No 477 of 2012 by Summons No 2990 of 2013 for leave to serve a third party notice against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity, and later sought to amend that notice to add a claim for contribution.

On 2013-04-19, in Suit No 1015 of 2012, Linda Kao Chai-Chau commenced third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity in respect of claims brought by Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.

On 2013-08-26, an application was made on behalf of Linda Kao Chai-Chau to amend the third party notice in Suit No 477 of 2012 to include an alternative claim for contribution and to state that Peter Fong, as a director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, owed the same duties as she did.

On 2013-09-02, Linda Kao Chai-Chau, through counsel, participated in a hearing of Registrar's Appeals No 246 of 2013 and No 247 of 2013 concerning her third party claims against the estate of Peter Fong.

On 2013-10-11, Linda Kao Chai-Chau, through counsel, made further submissions on legal questions relating to the effect of following instructions from a chairman who is said to be the controlling mind of a company.

case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

audit_suggestion.case_name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states that Linda was appointed as managing director of AT "some time in 1996" by PF.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the invented exact date with the year only.

fact_changes.evidence: The judgment says that "since 2006" PF had transferred the majority of his shares to the Fong Foundation charitable trust.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the invented exact date with the year only.

fact_changes.evidence: The judgment states that PF passed away on 25 April 2008.
fact_changes.recommended_change: No change needed to the date; however the fact should be narrowed to the source wording and not add unsupported role detail if not necessary.

fact_changes.evidence: The fact refers to the estate being named as intended third party and as third party in the 2013 proceedings, which is litigation-stage procedural history.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove this litigation-stage fact.

fact_changes.evidence: The fact concerns the estate not filing applications or objections during the 2013 appeals, which is procedural litigation conduct and post-dispute material.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove this procedural litigation fact.

facts_to_remove.current_value: By 2013, the estate of Peter Fong was named as the intended third party in Suit No 477 of 2012 and as third party in Suit No 1015 of 2012 in relation to potential indemnity or contribution claims by Linda Kao Chai-Chau.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is procedural litigation history from the 2013 third party proceedings and appeals.

facts_to_remove.current_value: Up to the time of the appeals in 2013, the estate of Peter Fong did not file applications to set aside the third party notices or to object to the commencement of third party proceedings in Suit No 477 of 2012 or Suit No 1015 of 2012.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is litigation-stage procedural conduct and not a neutral pre-dispute fact.

facts_to_remove.current_value: Whether the estate of Peter Fong may bear responsibility, alongside Linda Kao Chai-Chau, in respect of any loss suffered by Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd from the transactions in issue, such that contribution or indemnity between them is available.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This frames the legal issue in the appeal and reflects litigation-stage material.

facts_to_remove.current_value: If more than one person is legally responsible in respect of the same damage suffered by a company, statutory contribution provisions allow one such person to seek contribution from another, and third party procedures enable these questions to be determined within the same proceedings.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is court-level legal reasoning and procedural doctrine from the judgment.

facts_to_remove.current_value: The estate of Peter Fong is joined or intended to be joined as a third party in Suit No 477 of 2012 and Suit No 1015 of 2012 so that, if both Peter Fong and Linda Kao Chai-Chau are ultimately found responsible in respect of the same loss to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, the allocation of that loss between them can be addressed through contribution or indemnity within those suits.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This describes the litigation application and its purpose, which is post-dispute procedural material.

facts_to_remove.current_value: The judgment notes that the estate of Peter Fong, though separately represented, did not participate in the applications to set aside or oppose the third party proceedings. The court considers whether, on the assumed facts, Peter Fong could be responsible in respect of the same damage as Linda Kao Chai-Chau, and concludes that this is a realistic possibility. The court reasons that if both the managing director and the Chairman caused loss to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd through the transactions in question, statutory contribution between them may arise. The court therefore permits the third party proceedings against the estate of Peter Fong to continue and allows amendments to the third party notices, leaving the determination of any responsibility of the estate and any contribution to the trial court after full evidence is heard.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is entirely judgment reasoning and outcome material.
